claim: a newcomer's Bayesian surprise tracks the recent novelty rate, not just standing mass or bare newness; the constant reserve cannot tell drought from flurry, the signal reserve can
stimulus_shape: drought vs flurry, matched standing mass and surface, with a re-confirm control
mechanism: Born reserve fed the γ-decayed first-appearance mass ν through the unchanged Born step (context at the amplitude, not the law)
parity: 674 tests green; flag off byte-identical to the goldens
scope_holds: any reading with newcomers vs a non-trivial prior, both senses, across casts/lengths/γ
scope_fails: inert (correctly) on pure-confirmation readings with no newcomers

claim: the reserve tracks the RECENT rate, not a cumulative count — a stale flurry surprises its newcomer like a drought
stimulus_shape: stale flurry (early newcomers, then a confirmation stretch, then a newcomer)
mechanism: the γ decay term of ν′=γ·ν+newcomerMass is load-bearing; without it ν would never fall

claim: the dissociation is content- and parameter-independent
stimulus_shape: drought-vs-flurry across independent casts, lengths {4,6,8}, γ {0.5,0.7,0.9}
mechanism: PR-0001 mechanism on fresh random-seeded content

claim: the −log p surface channel carries the same constant reserve (pNovel) and is also blind to the novelty rate
stimulus_shape: drought-vs-flurry read on the surprisal channel
mechanism: would mirror EVO-1 on the surprisal reserve
scope_holds: surprisal(drought)=surprisal(flurry)=1.98 (blind) — confirms the surface cannot split; the fix is a future pressure

claim: forwardDist's generation reserve is the same constant; the draw's openness should track the recent novelty rate
scope_holds: open pressure for a later cycle

claim: with no newcomers the reserve is never drawn → ON==OFF
mechanism: correct inertness — the absence of the phenomenon
scope_holds: records a flat region — stop generating here
